title: T-006 — Baseline schema migration for tenants/control plane plus org-scoped organizations/users with R13 identity columns
prompt:
# T-006 — Baseline schema migration for tenants/control plane plus org-scoped organizations/users with R13 identity columns

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §8, §10, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Create the baseline Drizzle schema while separating tenant/control-plane concerns from org-scoped business/application rows.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T1-schema`.

## Implementation contract
Author `packages/db/schema/baseline.ts` and migration `001-baseline.sql`. `tenants` is control-plane: id UUID PK, name, region_cluster CHECK ('eu','us'), data_plane_url, created_at. `organizations` is the canonical business scope root: id UUID PK (this is `org_id` everywhere else), tenant_id nullable/required FK to tenants only as control-plane parent, name, industry_code CHECK ('bakery','pharma','fmcg','generic'), external_id, created_at, created_by_user, created_by_device, app_version, model_prediction_id, epcis_event_id, schema_version. `users` belongs to an org: id UUID PK, org_id UUID NOT NULL FK organizations(id), email CITEXT unique per org, display_name, external_id, created_at, created_by_user, created_by_device, app_version, model_prediction_id, epcis_event_id, schema_version. Business-facing indexes use `org_id`; do not add `tenant_id` to `users`. Integration tests query information_schema and constraints.

## Files in scope
- packages/db/schema/baseline.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/001-baseline.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/baseline.integration.test.ts [create]

## Dependencies
- T-002

## Cross-module dependencies
- None

## Acceptance criteria
1. A fresh migration creates tenants, organizations, and users; organizations.id is the org identifier and users has `org_id NOT NULL`.
2. No business/application table in this migration uses `tenant_id` as its RLS/business scope column except organizations.tenant_id as control-plane parent to tenants.
3. organizations.industry_code rejects unknown values at DB level.
4. Integration test asserts the R13 columns on organizations/users: id/external_id/org_id-or-id/created_at/created_by_user/created_by_device/app_version/model_prediction_id/epcis_event_id/schema_version as applicable.

## Test strategy
- RED: write baseline.integration.test.ts for org_id shape and R13 column presence before schema exists.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test` or the package's integration test command.

## Risk red lines
- Do not make `tenant_id` the business scope for users or future business tables.
- Do not add RLS policies here; T-007 owns RLS.
- Do not create NPD/Technical domain tables here.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
